author: codex-root

patch.value:
## Public surface

- RelationshipKindRegistry and schema validation.
- Append, remove or supersede, and inspect relationship events.
- Snapshot-aware graph reader and index interfaces.
- Bounded adjacency, path, closure, impact, and subgraph queries.
- Explain and cost metadata suitable for token-aware context selection.
- Import, export, merge, and extension hooks built from the same contract.

## Non-goals

This item does not choose product-specific relevance weights, silently invent relationships, or force large indexes on scratch projects. Those belong to context-engine policy, audits, and adaptive storage layers.
SDK relationship registry and graph-query primitives: custom typed edges, adjacency, paths, closures, impact, and bounded subgraphs
Promote relationship definition, mutation, indexing, and bounded traversal into stable public SDK APIs so every adapter and domain model shares one implementation.

The registry defines built-in and extension-provided kinds with direction, inverse, order-bearing and hierarchy flags, cardinality, payload schema, lifecycle, aliases, and compatibility version; Mutation APIs validate endpoints and invariants then append attributable immutable events without direct state rewrites; Read APIs cover adjacency, predecessors, successors, ancestors, descendants, shortest and constrained paths, transitive closure, reverse impact, and induced bounded subgraphs; Every query supports deterministic ordering, typed filters, field projection, cursor or streaming continuation, cancellation, snapshot selection, and explicit cost or truncation metadata; Indexes are rebuildable from history, incrementally maintained, crash safe, observable, and optional for small projects; Thin CLI and MCP adapters call public SDK APIs without hidden core imports; shell-safe JSON, JSONL, TOON, and counts-first outputs preserve pipeline composability; Contract, property, replay, concurrency, migration, and scale tests cover legacy kinds, custom kinds, cycles, snapshots, and one million items
Make the public SDK the complete source of truth for relationship schemas and graph operations
CLI, MCP, extensions, scripts, and non-PM applications can build on one deterministic and extensible graph contract
Removes adapter-local graph logic and unlocks typed domain relationships, scalable context assembly, and future graph algorithms
Versioned SDK APIs for kind registries, immutable mutations, adjacency and path queries, closures, impact traversal, and bounded subgraphs
The CLI has dependency operations and visualization, but custom semantic kinds and complete public graph primitives lack a canonical SDK contract

value.author: codex-root

message: Track test run summary (test-local-mrkckbng-g4g06q)

value.author: codex-gpt5-root
value.text: Evidence: delivered the first broad public graph foundation: versioned built-in/custom relationship registry, alias compatibility, immutable in-memory index construction from item metadata, deterministic adjacency/closure/reverse-impact/shortest-path/subgraph queries with cancellation and explicit cost/truncation metadata, SDK barrel/docs, and validate integration. Packed tarball consumer proved a custom company owns graph plus real CLI lifecycle. Full repository run passed 286 files/5664 tests; focused all-source coverage for relationships.ts is 100/100/100/100. Remaining event mutation, durable incremental index, snapshot/streaming, MCP projection, and million-item scale acceptance stay on this feature, so it will return to open rather than be falsely closed.

value.author: codex-gpt5-root
value.text: A storage-independent relationship registry is the semantic boundary: algorithms must consume ordering/hierarchy flags rather than infer meaning from labels, while durable event/index implementations can remain interchangeable.

Regression tests cover non-monotonic replay, empty pre-history snapshots, active-count independence, and cardinality-slot reuse. Verification: 287/287 files and 5,683/5,683 tests passed with exact 100% statements/branches/functions/lines (36,684/26,338/7,410/35,813); lint, typecheck, jscpd zero clones, 100% docstring gate, package boundary, and token-budget gates passed. Gemini threads PRRT_kwDORSgRDc6Qutwn and PRRT_kwDORSgRDc6Qutwt were reacted to, replied to, and resolved. Item remains open for later durable-adapter work; active implementation is complete for this tranche.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Adapter-acceptance progress via the pm-8xr8 lane (2026-07-17): the graph kernel gained the public neighborEdges(id, options) primitive (deterministic neighbor rows paired with their connecting edges - the seam context explanation needed), and the native CLI/MCP graph-context adapter now exposes direction and registry-validated kind filters end-to-end with contract/schema/completion parity. Remaining canonical scope here is unchanged: durable incremental indexes, migration/backfill, JSONL/stream projections, deeper crash recovery, and million-item storage validation (pm-m2il matrix).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Implementation constraints recorded 2026-07-17 (review pass #96), two hard facts for whoever claims this slice. (1) src/sdk/runtime.ts gate headroom is 2 lines (census on pm-yh8r) — this slice's registry/graph-query exports CANNOT go through runtime.ts; ship the runtime partition first/with it, or route the new primitives through a dedicated subpath module (precedent: PR#571-lane sdk/test + sdk/diagnostics folders; sdk/query/ is the natural home). (2) Perf shape to fix while building the query primitives: pm deps --format context today assembles the FULL workspace RelationshipGraph + dangling-reference collection on every call (assembleDepsRelationshipGraph in src/sdk/dependencies.ts calls collectDanglingDependencyReferences + RelationshipGraph.fromItems over ALL items before bounding) — bounded output but unbounded assembly cost, O(all items) per invocation. The adjacency/bounded-subgraph primitives owned here are the natural place to make assembly incremental/indexed so bounded queries stop paying full-workspace cost; pm-m2il's scale matrix (query-budget axis) will measure it, pm-9rxu bl8x cache contract (CACHE_VERSION) is the substrate if an index is persisted.
